Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server

Annotation Interface Rest

@Target(TYPE) @Retention(RUNTIME) @Inherited @AnnotationGroup(Rest.class) public @interface Rest
Used to denote that a class is a REST resource and to associate metadata on it.

Usually used on a subclass of RestServlet, but can be used to annotate any class that you want to expose as a REST resource.

See Also:
  • @Rest-Annotated Class Basics

  • Optional Element Summary

    Optional Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Optional Element
    Description
    String
    allowedHeaderParams
    Allowed header URL parameters.
    String
    allowedMethodHeaders

    Allowed method parameters.
    String[]

    Allowed parser session option keys for this resource (ordered merge, prefix -key removes a key).
    String[]

    Allowed serializer session option keys for this resource (ordered merge, prefix -key removes a key).

    asyncCompletionExecutor
    Names the Executor bean used to route CompletableFuture completion callbacks through a dedicated thread pool.

    Configurable timeout (milliseconds) applied to CompletableFuture-returning handlers by AsyncResponseProcessor.

    The maximum allowed input size (in bytes) on HTTP requests.

    Property names for which less-derived contributions are NOT inherited.

    observability
    Per-resource observability opt-in / opt-out control.

    openApiProvider
    OpenAPI 3.1 provider.

    parsers
    Specifies the parsers for converting HTTP request bodies into POJOs.

    HTTP part parser.

    partSerializer
    HTTP part serializer.

    Resource path.

    pathParams
    Default path parameter definitions.

    Additional servlet mount paths.

    Opt the resource into RFC 7807 / RFC 9457 application/problem+json error responses.

    produces
    Supported accept media types.
    Query[]
    queryParams
    Default query parameter definitions.
    String
    renderResponseStackTraces
    Render response stack traces in responses.

    responseProcessors
    Response processors.

    restOpArgs
    Java REST operation method parameter resolvers.

    roleGuard
    Role guard.
    String
    rolesDeclared
    Declared roles.
    Class<? extends Serializer>[]
    serializers
    Specifies the serializers for POJOs into HTTP response bodies.

    siteName
    Optional site name.

    staticFiles

    swagger
    Provides swagger-specific metadata on this resource.

    swaggerProvider

    Optional servlet title.

    Resource context path.

    String

    String
    virtualThreads

  • Element Details

    • allowedHeaderParams

      String allowedHeaderParams
      Allowed header URL parameters.

      When specified, allows headers such as "Accept" and "Content-Type" to be passed in as URL query parameters.
      For example:

      ?Accept=text/json&Content-Type=text/json

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • Use "*" to represent all methods.
      • Use "NONE" (case insensitive) to suppress inheriting a value from a parent class.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • allowedMethodHeaders

      String allowedMethodHeaders
      Allowed method headers.

      A comma-delimited list of HTTP method names that are allowed to be passed as values in an X-Method HTTP header to override the real HTTP method name.

      Allows you to override the actual HTTP method with a simulated method.
      For example, if an HTTP Client API doesn't support PATCH but does support POST (because PATCH is not part of the original HTTP spec), you can add a X-Method: PATCH header on a normal HTTP POST /foo request call which will make the HTTP call look like a PATCH request in any of the REST APIs.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • Method names are case-insensitive.
      • Use "*" to represent all methods.
      • Use "NONE" (case insensitive) to suppress inheriting a value from a parent class.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • allowedMethodParams

      String allowedMethodParams
      Allowed method parameters.

      When specified, the HTTP method can be overridden by passing in a "method" URL parameter on a regular GET request.
      For example:

      ?method=OPTIONS

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • Use "*" to represent all methods.
      • Use "NONE" (case insensitive) to suppress inheriting a value from a parent class.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • callLogger

      Class<? extends CallLogger> callLogger
      Specifies the logger to use for logging of HTTP requests and responses.
      Notes:
      • The default call logger if not specified is CallLogger.
      • The resource class itself will be used if it implements the CallLogger interface and not explicitly overridden via this annotation.
      • The implementation must have one of the following constructors:
        • public T(RestContext)
        • public T()
        • public static T create(RestContext)
        • public static T create()
      • Inner classes of the REST resource class are allowed.
      See Also:
      • Logging / Debugging
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.rest.server.logger.CallLogger.Void.class

    • authenticator

      Class<? extends RestAuthenticator> authenticator
      Resource-level authenticator.

      Resolves the Principal and roles for requests to this resource and its descendant child resources (inherited unless cut off via noInherit={"authenticator"}), so that roleGuard / RoleBasedRestGuard / @Auth / isUserInRole resolve against the computed identity.

      Notes:
      • The implementation is instantiated through the resource's bean store (constructor injection).
      • The default RestAuthenticator.Null sentinel means "not specified."
      See Also:
      • REST Authenticator
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      10.0.0
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.RestAuthenticator.Null.class

    • children

      Class<?>[] children
      REST children.

      Defines children of this resource. Each child resource is mounted under its own URL subtree (per the child's own @Rest(path=...) / @Rest(paths=...)) and constructs its own RestContext.

      Children vs. mixins (resolution semantics)

      Children are isolated from the parent's resolution chain by design — a child's serializers, parsers, guards, hooks, call-logger, etc. are all resolved against the child's own RestContext only, NOT walked through the parent. This is the opposite of how mixins resolve. See the Mixin Sub-Contexts — Mixin-vs-child divergence topic for the rationale (children own their lifecycle and are externally mounted; mixins are inline composers sharing the host's URL namespace and resolution chain).

      Inheritance Rules
      • Children on child are combined with those on parent class.
      • Children are list parent-to-child in the order they appear in the annotation.
      See Also:
      • Child Resources
      • Mixin Sub-Contexts (for the mixin-vs-child divergence)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • mixins

      Class<?>[] mixins
      REST mixins.

      Defines operation-provider classes whose @RestOp-group methods should be composed into this resource.

      Mixin methods are discovered the same way as local operation methods and surface under this resource's URL namespace. On path/method collisions, local methods on this resource win over mixin methods.

      Per-mixin RestContext + host-to-mixin inheritance (since 10.0.0)

      Each mixin class is elevated to its own RestContext parent-linked to this host's RestContext. The mixin's class-level @Rest(...) configuration applies to its own endpoints, with inheritance from the host:

      • List-shaped properties (serializers, parsers, encoders, converters, responseProcessors, restOpArgs, guards) — host's chain runs first, then the mixin's appended. Host endpoints see only the host's chain.
      • Replace-shaped properties (callLogger, debugEnablement, debugDefault, partSerializer, partParser) — the mixin's value wins over the host's for mixin endpoints when declared; otherwise the host's value is inherited.
      • messages — the mixin's bundle is chained as a child of the host's via Messages.chain(child, parent) so mixin keys win and missing keys fall through to the host.
      • Lifecycle hooks (@RestStartCall, @RestEndCall, @RestPreCall, @RestPostCall, @RestDestroy) — dual-fire host-then-mixin for mixin-endpoint requests; host-only for host-endpoint requests.

      Use @Rest(noInherit={...}) on a mixin class to cut off inheritance for a specific property — same token set as the host's noInherit machinery (e.g. "serializers", "guards", "messages").

      Mixins vs. children

      Mixins are inline composers that share the host's URL namespace and inherit from the host's resolution chain. Children are independently-mounted resources isolated from the host's resolution chain. See the Mixin Sub-Contexts — Mixin-vs-child divergence topic for the rationale.

      Inheritance Rules
      • Mixins on child are combined with those on parent class.
      • Mixins are listed parent-to-child in the order they appear in the annotation.
      • Transitive mixins (A mixes in B) parent-link flat to the host — both A and B get parentContext = host, never an A → B chain.

      For host-side per-mixin setting overrides (applying guards / serializers / a mount path / etc. to a specific mixin's endpoints without editing the mixin class), see the rich mixinDefs companion attribute.

      See Also:
      • mixinDefs()
      • REST Server — Mixins and Multi-Mount Paths
      • REST Server — Mixin Sub-Contexts
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • mixinDefs

      Mixin[] mixinDefs
      Rich mixin definitions — the host-side override form of mixins().

      Each @Mixin entry names a mixin class (via Mixin.type()) and lets the host override selected @Rest-level settings for that mixin's mixed-in endpoints — guards, role guards, serializers, default headers, mount path, etc. — without subclassing or editing the mixin class.

      Example:

      @Rest( mixinDefs=@Mixin(type=FooMixin.class, guards=AdminGuard.class) ) public class MyResource { ... }

      This attribute is additive to mixins(): bare-class entries in mixins() and rich entries here are discovered together (bare classes first, then mixinDefs). A @Mixin(type=X.class) with no overrides is the exact equivalent of a bare mixins=X.class entry.

      See Also:
      • Mixin
      • mixins()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      10.0.0
      Default:
      {}

    • clientVersionHeader

      String clientVersionHeader
      Client version header.

      Specifies the name of the header used to denote the client version on HTTP requests.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • config

      String config
      Optional location of configuration file for this servlet.

      The configuration file .

      Inheritance Rules
      • Config file is searched for in child-to-parent order.
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • Use the keyword SYSTEM_DEFAULT to refer to the system default configuration returned by the Config.getSystemDefault().
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • eagerInit

      String eagerInit
      Eagerly initializes framework-managed memoizers during RestContext construction.

      When enabled, framework bean memoizers and operation/child-context memoizers are force-fired inside the constructor try/catch so startup-time configuration errors fail fast.

      This setting is disabled by default.

      • "true" - Force eager initialization during context construction.
      • "false" - Keep initialization lazy until first use (default).
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • lazyChildren

      String lazyChildren
      Opt this parent resource into deferred (first-invocation) construction of its children() sub-resources.

      When "true", the RestContext instances for all @Rest(children=...) entries are not built at parent startup. Instead, each child's routing entry is registered immediately (so URL matching is fully operational from the first request), but the full RestContext — including all of its bean-store setup, memoizers, and lifecycle hooks — is constructed on the first inbound request to that child's URL prefix. Subsequent requests reuse the already-built context.

      This is particularly useful when a parent resource exposes heavyweight admin or diagnostic children that are rarely invoked in production. Setting lazyChildren="true" on the parent lets the parent boot fast; each child pays its construction cost only when (and if) it is first accessed.

      • "true" - Children are constructed on first invocation (deferred).
      • "false" (default) - Children are constructed eagerly at parent startup.
      Notes:
      • The first request to a lazy child pays the full construction cost, which can be significant for heavyweight children. If predictable first-request latency is required, do not opt in.
      • Concurrent first-requests to the same lazy child are serialized: only one thread runs the construction; others block until it completes.
      • A lazy child that is never invoked is never constructed. Its lifecycle @RestDestroy / shutdown hooks are skipped at parent destruction time.
      • The programmatic knob RestContext.Builder.lazyChildInit(boolean) overrides this annotation.
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$E{LAZY_CHILDREN,false}").
      See Also:
      • RestContext.Builder.lazyChildInit(boolean)
      • children()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • consumes

      String[] consumes
      Supported content media types.

      Overrides the media types inferred from the parsers that identify what media types can be consumed by the resource.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • allowedSerializerOptions

      String[] allowedSerializerOptions
      Allowed serializer session option keys for this resource (ordered merge, prefix -key removes a key).

      Comma-delimited list of session property keys (e.g. "escapeSolidus,maxIndent") that clients may send via the "X-Juneau-Serializer-Options" header or "juneauSerializerOptions" query parameter. Keys not in the effective allowlist cause a 400 Bad Request response.

      Entries are merged in application order. A leading hyphen removes a previously added key: "-escapeSolidus". Method-level RestGet.allowedSerializerOptions() values are always merged on top. Use noInherit() to prevent inheriting less-derived contributions.

      See Also:
      • Session Options - Safe Properties
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • allowedParserOptions

      String[] allowedParserOptions
      Allowed parser session option keys for this resource (ordered merge, prefix -key removes a key).

      Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the "X-Juneau-Parser-Options" header or "juneauParserOptions" query parameter. Parser options are ignored for operations without a request-body parser. Keys not in the effective allowlist cause a 400 Bad Request response.

      Use noInherit() to prevent inheriting less-derived contributions.

      See Also:
      • Session Options - Safe Properties
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • noInherit

      String[] noInherit
      Property names for which less-derived contributions are NOT inherited.

      Accepted values include "allowedParserOptions", "allowedSerializerOptions", "allowedHeaderParams", "allowedMethodHeaders", "allowedMethodParams", "disableContentParam", "renderResponseStackTraces", "problemDetails", "eagerInit", "lazyChildren", "clientVersionHeader", "uriAuthority", "uriContext", "uriRelativity", and "uriResolution". Each entry is SVL-resolved then comma-split. Prevents the named property from inheriting values from parent @Rest annotations (router hierarchy). The noInherit attribute itself is never inherited.

      Mixin sub-contexts (since 10.0.0)

      On a class declared via @Rest(mixins=...) on a host, noInherit also blocks the host-to-mixin inheritance walk for the named property. The token set extends to every contribution list exposed by @Rest: "serializers", "parsers", "encoders", "converters", "responseProcessors", "restOpArgs", "guards", "callLogger", "debugEnablement", "debugDefault", "partSerializer", "partParser", and "messages". For example, @Rest(noInherit={"guards"}) on a mixin removes the host's guard chain from the mixin's endpoints (typical pattern for deliberately-unguarded probes like HealthMixin).

      See Also:
      • Session Options
      • REST Server — Mixin Sub-Contexts (for the per-property opt-out semantics)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • converters

      Class<? extends RestConverter>[] converters
      Class-level response converters.

      Associates one or more converters with a resource class.

      Inheritance Rules
      • Converters on child are combined with those on parent class.
      • Converters are executed child-to-parent in the order they appear in the annotation.
      • Converters on methods are executed before those on classes.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • debug

      Debug debug
      Enable debug mode.

      Enables the following:

      • HTTP request/response bodies are cached in memory for logging purposes.
      • HTTP requests/responses are logged to the registered CallLogger.
      • "true" - Debug is enabled for all requests.
      • "false" - Debug is disabled for all requests.
      • "conditional" - Debug is enabled only for requests that have a Debug: true header.
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • These debug settings can be overridden by the debug() annotation or at runtime by directly calling RestRequest.setDebug().
      See Also:
      • RestContext.getDebugEnablement()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      @org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Debug

    • defaultAccept

      String defaultAccept
      Default Accept header.

      The default value for the Accept header if not specified on a request.

      This is a shortcut for using defaultRequestHeaders() for just this specific header.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • defaultCharset

      String defaultCharset
      Default character encoding.

      The default character encoding for the request and response if not specified on the request.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      See Also:
      • RestOp.defaultCharset()
      • RestGet.defaultCharset()
      • RestPut.defaultCharset()
      • RestPost.defaultCharset()
      • RestDelete.defaultCharset()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • defaultContentType

      String defaultContentType
      Default Content-Type header.

      The default value for the Content-Type header if not specified on a request.

      This is a shortcut for using defaultRequestHeaders() for just this specific header.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • defaultRequestAttributes

      String[] defaultRequestAttributes
      Default request attributes.

      Specifies default values for request attributes if they're not already set on the request.

      Affects values returned by the following methods:

      • RestRequest.getAttribute(String).
      • RestRequest.getAttributes().
      Example:

      // Defined via annotation resolving to a config file setting with default value. @Rest(defaultRequestAttributes={"Foo=bar", "Baz: $C{REST/myAttributeValue}"}) public class MyResource { // Override at the method level. @RestGet(defaultRequestAttributes={"Foo: bar"}) public Object myMethod() {...} }

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      See Also:
      • RestOp.defaultRequestAttributes()
      • RestGet.defaultRequestAttributes()
      • RestPut.defaultRequestAttributes()
      • RestPost.defaultRequestAttributes()
      • RestDelete.defaultRequestAttributes()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • defaultRequestHeaders

      String[] defaultRequestHeaders
      Default request headers.

      Specifies default values for request headers if they're not passed in through the request.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • defaultResponseHeaders

      String[] defaultResponseHeaders
      Default response headers.

      Specifies default values for response headers if they're not set after the Java REST method is called.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • description

      String[] description
      Optional servlet description.

      It is used to populate the Swagger description field.

      Inheritance Rules
      • Description is searched for in child-to-parent order.
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • The format is plain-text.
        Multiple lines are concatenated with newlines.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • disableContentParam

      String disableContentParam
      Disable content URL parameter.

      When enabled, the HTTP content content on PUT and POST requests can be passed in as text using the "content" URL parameter.
      For example:

      ?content=(name='John%20Smith',age=45)

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • problemDetails

      String problemDetails
      Opt the resource into RFC 7807 / RFC 9457 application/problem+json error responses.

      When enabled, the resource:

      • Emits application/problem+json for thrown BasicHttpExceptions — regardless of the client's Accept header. The body is a serialized Problem populated from the exception's status code, reason phrase, and message.
      • Honors the client's Accept header on the success path. When a @RestOp method returns a Problem (or throws a ProblemException), the ProblemDetailsProcessor only serializes it as application/problem+json when the Accept header matches that media type (or *&#47;*).
      • "true" — enable problem-details responses for this resource.
      • "false" — disable problem-details responses for this resource (overrides an inherited "true" from a parent @Rest).
      • "" (default) — inherit from the next-most-derived @Rest in the resource-class hierarchy. Default behavior (no opt-in anywhere in the chain) is unchanged from prior releases.
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • Use noInherit() to prevent inheriting an opt-in from a parent @Rest.
      See Also:
      • Problem
      • ProblemException
      • ProblemDetailsProcessor
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • virtualThreads

      String virtualThreads
      Opt this resource into per-request virtual-thread dispatch (Java 21+).

      When enabled, every @RestOp-annotated handler invocation on this resource is submitted to a Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor() virtual-thread-per-task executor lazily built by the RestContext. The platform request thread blocks on the virtual thread's completion (so the handler's return value, exceptions, and observability hooks are preserved verbatim), but blocking I/O inside the handler now parks a virtual thread instead of the carrier — i.e. the carrier thread is freed to service other concurrent requests while the handler is parked on socket / file / lock waits. Combined with CompletableFuture return types (@RestGet / @RestPost returning CompletableFuture<T>) this is the high-throughput pattern.

      Graceful degradation on Java 17/18/19/20: the flag is detected during RestContext initialization. If the runtime is older than Java 21, a one-shot WARNING is logged and the resource falls back to the standard caller-thread dispatch path — no runtime error.

      Per-@RestOp overrides are available via RestOp.virtualThreads(); the op-level setting takes precedence over the resource-level setting.

      • "true" — enable virtual-thread dispatch on Java 21+ (silently disabled on older JVMs).
      • "false" — explicitly disable.
      • "" (default) — inherit from the next-most-derived @Rest in the resource-class hierarchy.
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$E{ENABLE_VIRTUAL_THREADS,false}").
      • Synchronized blocks and JNI calls in handler code pin a virtual thread to its carrier thread — prefer ReentrantLock over synchronized in handlers run under this flag.
      See Also:
      • RestOp.virtualThreads()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • observability

      String observability
      Per-resource observability opt-in / opt-out control.

      Controls whether the observability block (MetricsRecorder / TracerHook) fires for operations on this resource. Per-operation overrides are available via RestOp.observability() (and the verb annotations).

      • "true" — strict opt-in: the resource requires a wired observability backend. If neither a @Bean MetricsRecorder nor a @Bean TracerHook is registered when the RestContext is built, construction fails with a precise error. All operations on this resource have observability enabled.
      • "false" — explicit opt-out: the observability block is short-circuited for every operation on this resource, even when a wired backend is present. Also suppresses TraceContextResponseProcessor header injection for this resource.
      • "" (default) — inherits the existing behavior: the observability block runs per-op, but a missing @Bean silently falls back to the NoOp singleton — no startup error.
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$E{ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY,false}").
      • Per-operation overrides via RestOp.observability() (or verb annotations) take precedence over this resource-level setting.
      See Also:
      • RestOp.observability()
      • MetricsRecorder
      • TracerHook
      • REST Server — Observability
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      10.0.0
      Default:
      ""

    • asyncTimeoutMillis

      String asyncTimeoutMillis
      Configurable timeout (milliseconds) applied to CompletableFuture-returning handlers by AsyncResponseProcessor. Default is 30,000 ms.

      On timeout, the future is cancelled with mayInterruptIfRunning=true and the response is committed as 504 Gateway Timeout. Set to "0" to disable the timeout entirely.

      Per-@RestOp overrides are available via RestOp.asyncTimeoutMillis(); the op-level setting takes precedence over the resource-level setting.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$E{ASYNC_TIMEOUT_MS,30000}").
      See Also:
      • AsyncResponseProcessor
      • RestOp.asyncTimeoutMillis()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      10.0.0
      Default:
      ""

    • asyncCompletionExecutor

      String asyncCompletionExecutor
      Names the Executor bean used to route CompletableFuture completion callbacks through a dedicated thread pool.

      When set, the AsyncResponseProcessor switches from future.whenComplete(callback) to future.whenCompleteAsync(callback, executor), so the response-handler work runs on the named pool instead of the future's natural completion thread (often an I/O-driver or database-callback thread).

      The value is the @Bean(name=...) lookup key for an Executor registered in the resource's bean store. Empty string (default) means no override — completion callbacks run on the future's natural thread (the existing behavior).

      Example:

      @Rest(path="/api", asyncCompletionExecutor="myCompletionPool") public class ApiResource extends RestServlet { @Bean(name="myCompletionPool") public Executor completionPool() { return Executors.newFixedThreadPool(8); } @RestGet("/orders/{id}") public CompletableFuture<Order> getOrder(@Path String id) { return orderService.fetchAsync(id); // callback runs on myCompletionPool } }

      Per-operation overrides are available via RestOp.asyncCompletionExecutor() (and the verb annotations). A reference to a bean name that does not resolve in the bean store causes a startup failure.

      Notes:
      • MDC propagation still works when this executor is set — the MdcAsyncListener wraps the callback before it is routed through the executor, so the MDC snapshot is restored on whichever thread the executor picks.
      See Also:
      • AsyncResponseProcessor
      • RestOp.asyncCompletionExecutor()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      10.0.0
      Default:
      ""

    • encoders

      Class<? extends Encoder>[] encoders
      Specifies the compression encoders for this resource.

      Encoders are used to enable various kinds of compression (e.g. "gzip") on requests and responses.

      Encoders are automatically inherited from encoders() annotations on parent classes with the encoders on child classes prepended to the encoder group. The EncoderSet.NoInherit class can be used to prevent inheriting from the parent class.

      Example:

      // Define a REST resource that handles GZIP compression. @Rest( encoders={ GzipEncoder.class } ) public class MyResource { ... }

      The encoders can also be tailored at the method level using RestOp.encoders() (and related annotations).

      For programmatic equivalents, contribute an EncoderSet bean via @Bean(name="encoders").

      Inheritance Rules
      • Encoders on child are combined with those on parent class.
      See Also:
      • Encoders
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • formDataParams

      FormData[] formDataParams
      Default form data parameter definitions.

      Provides default values for @FormData annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class.

      Example:

      @Rest( formDataParams={ @FormData(name="action", def="submit") } ) public class MyResource {...}

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      9.2.0
      Default:
      {}

    • guards

      Class<? extends RestGuard>[] guards
      Class-level guards.

      Associates one or more RestGuards with all REST methods defined in this class.

      Inheritance Rules
      • Guards on child are combined with those on parent class.
      • Guards are executed child-to-parent in the order they appear in the annotation.
      • Guards on methods are executed before those on classes.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • headerParams

      Header[] headerParams
      Default header parameter definitions.

      Provides default values for @Header annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class.

      Example:

      @Rest( headerParams={ @Header(name="Accept-Language", def="en-US"), @Header(name="X-API-Version", def="1.0") } ) public class MyResource {...}

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      9.2.0
      Default:
      {}

    • maxInput

      String maxInput
      The maximum allowed input size (in bytes) on HTTP requests.

      Useful for alleviating DoS attacks by throwing an exception when too much input is received instead of resulting in out-of-memory errors which could affect system stability.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      See Also:
      • RestOp.maxInput()
      • RestPost.maxInput()
      • RestPut.maxInput()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • messages

      String messages
      Messages. Identifies the location of the resource bundle for this class.

      There are two possible formats:

      • A simple string - Represents the name of the resource bundle.

        Example:

        // Bundle name is Messages.properties. @Rest(messages="Messages")

      • Simplified JSON - Represents parameters for the Messages.Builder class.

        Example:

        // Bundles can be found in two packages. @Rest(messages="{name:'Messages',baseNames:['{package}.{name}','{package}.i18n.{name}']")

      If the bundle name is not specified, the class name of the resource object is used.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • parsers

      Class<?>[] parsers
      Specifies the parsers for converting HTTP request bodies into POJOs.

      Parsers are used to convert the content of HTTP requests into POJOs.
      Any of the Juneau framework parsers can be used in this setting.
      The parser selected is based on the request Content-Type header matched against the values returned by the following method using a best-match algorithm:

      • Parser.getMediaTypes()

      Parsers are automatically inherited from parsers() annotations on parent classes with the parsers on child classes prepended to the parser group. The ParserSet.NoInherit class can be used to prevent inheriting from the parent class.

      Example:

      // Define a REST resource that can consume JSON and XML. @Rest( parsers={ JsonParser.class, XmlParser.class } ) public class MyResource { ... }

      The parsers can also be tailored at the method level using RestOp.parsers() (and related annotations).

      For programmatic equivalents, contribute a ParserSet bean via @Bean(name="parsers").

      Inheritance Rules
      • Parsers on child override those on parent class.
      • ParserSet.Inherit class can be used to inherit and augment values from parent.
      • ParserSet.NoInherit class can be used to suppress inheriting from parent.
      • Parsers on methods take precedence over those on classes.
      See Also:
      • Marshalling
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • partParser

      Class<? extends HttpPartParser> partParser
      HTTP part parser.

      Specifies the HttpPartParser to use for parsing headers, query/form parameters, and URI parts.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.HttpPartParser.Void.class

    • partSerializer

      Class<? extends HttpPartSerializer> partSerializer
      HTTP part serializer.

      Specifies the HttpPartSerializer to use for serializing headers, query/form parameters, and URI parts.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.HttpPartSerializer.Void.class

    • path

      String path
      Resource path.

      Used in the following situations:

      • On child resources (resource classes attached to parents via the children() annotation) to identify the subpath used to access the child resource relative to the parent.
      • On top-level RestServlet classes deployed as Spring beans when JuneauRestInitializer is being used.
      On child resources

      The typical usage is to define a path to a child resource relative to the parent resource.

      Example:

      @Rest( children={ChildResource.class} ) public class TopLevelResource extends BasicRestServlet {...} @Rest( path="/child", children={GrandchildResource.class} ) public class ChildResource {...} @Rest( path="/grandchild" ) public class GrandchildResource { @RestGet("/") public String sayHello() { return "Hello!"; } }

      In the example above, assuming the TopLevelResource servlet is deployed to path /myContext/myServlet, then the sayHello method is accessible through the URI /myContext/myServlet/child/grandchild.

      Note that in this scenario, the path attribute is not defined on the top-level resource. Specifying the path on the top-level resource has no effect, but can be used for readability purposes.

      Path variables

      The path can contain variables that get resolved to @Path parameters or access through the RestRequest.getPathParams() method.

      Example:

      @Rest( path="/myResource/{foo}/{bar}" ) public class MyResource extends BasicRestServlet { @RestGet("/{baz}") public void String doX(@Path String foo, @Path int bar, @Path MyPojo baz) { ... } }

      Variables can be used on either top-level or child resources and can be defined on multiple levels.

      All variables in the path must be specified or else the target will not resolve and a 404 will result.

      When variables are used on a path of a top-level resource deployed as a Spring bean in a Spring Boot application, the first part of the URL must be a literal which will be used as the servlet path of the registered servlet.

      Notes:
      • The leading slash is optional. "/myResource" and "myResource" is equivalent.
      • The paths "/myResource" and "/myResource/*" are equivalent.
      • Paths must not end with "/" (per the servlet spec).
      • Effective only when this class is registered directly with the servlet container as a top-level resource. When this class is imported as a mixin via @Rest(mixins=...), the importing host's own path() or paths() governs the mount and this annotation is silently ignored — mixin endpoints land in the host's URL namespace.
      Inheritance Rules
      • Path is searched for in child-to-parent order.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • paths

      String[] paths
      Additional servlet mount paths.

      Optional multi-mount companion to path() for top-level servlet deployment.

      When specified, servlet containers may mount this resource on each listed path. This is primarily intended for built-in support endpoints such as health probes where multiple exact URLs should be served by a single servlet instance.

      Runtime substitution (since 10.0.0)

      Each array element is treated as a template:

      1. The element runs through SVL variable substitution (e.g. $C{health.paths}, $E{HEALTH_PATHS,/healthz}, $S{my.system.prop,/healthz}) using the bootstrap VarResolver on the bean store.
      2. The post-SVL value is split on ,; each piece is trimmed and empty pieces are dropped. A single template element can therefore expand to zero, one, or many mount paths.
      Examples:

      // 1. Single key resolving to a multi-value config string. @Rest(paths={"$C{health.paths}"}) // 2. Mix literal + resolved element. @Rest(paths={"/api", "$C{extra.paths}"}) // 3. Env var with comma-separated defaults baked in. @Rest(paths={"$E{HEALTH_PATHS,/healthz,/readyz}"})

      Notes:
      • Paths are normalized to servlet path-specs by the hosting runtime.
      • When both path() and paths() are present, runtimes may use paths() for top-level mounting and continue using path() for child-resource composition.
      • The annotation default sits at the lowest rung of the runtime-override resolution chain — see RestContext.getPaths() for the full precedence order (programmatic > getter > annotation).
      • An SVL failure (unresolved variable with no default) falls back to the literal element rather than throwing during construction.
      • Effective only when this class is registered directly with the servlet container as a top-level resource. When this class is imported as a mixin via @Rest(mixins=...), the importing host's own path() or paths() governs the mount and this annotation is silently ignored — mixin endpoints land in the host's URL namespace.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • pathParams

      Path[] pathParams
      Default path parameter definitions.

      Provides default values for @Path annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class.

      Example:

      @Rest( pathParams={ @Path(name="version", def="v1") } ) public class MyResource {...}

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      9.2.0
      Default:
      {}

    • produces

      String[] produces
      Supported accept media types.

      Overrides the media types inferred from the serializers that identify what media types can be produced by the resource.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • queryParams

      Query[] queryParams
      Default query parameter definitions.

      Provides default values for @Query annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class. Values specified here are used as defaults when the same property is not explicitly defined on a method parameter's @Query annotation.

      Example:

      // Define common query parameters at class level @Rest( queryParams={ @Query(name="format", def="json", description="Output format"), @Query(name="verbose", def="false", schema=@Schema(type="boolean")) } ) public class MyResource { // Method will inherit the default query parameter definitions @RestGet public String doGet(@Query("format") String format, @Query("verbose") boolean verbose) {...} // Can override defaults on a per-method basis @RestGet public String doGet2(@Query(name="format", def="xml") String format) {...} }

      Notes:
      • These defaults apply to validation, parsing, and OpenAPI/Swagger documentation generation.
      • Method-level @Query annotations take precedence over class-level definitions.
      • The name attribute must be specified for each query definition.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      9.2.0
      Default:
      {}

    • renderResponseStackTraces

      String renderResponseStackTraces
      Render response stack traces in responses.

      Render stack traces in HTTP response bodies when errors occur.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • responseProcessors

      Class<? extends ResponseProcessor>[] responseProcessors
      Response processors.

      Specifies a list of ResponseProcessor classes that know how to convert POJOs returned by REST methods or set via RestResponse.setContent(Object) into appropriate HTTP responses.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • restOpArgs

      Class<? extends RestOpArg>[] restOpArgs
      Java REST operation method parameter resolvers.

      By default, the Juneau framework will automatically Java method parameters of various types (e.g. RestRequest, Accept, Reader).
      This setting allows you to provide your own resolvers for your own class types that you want resolved.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • roleGuard

      String roleGuard
      Role guard.

      An expression defining if a user with the specified roles are allowed to access methods on this class.

      This is a shortcut for specifying RestOp.roleGuard() on all the REST operations on a class.

      Example:

      @Rest( path="/foo", roleGuard="ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE && ROLE_SPECIAL)" ) public class MyResource extends BasicRestServlet { ... }

      Notes:
      • Supports any of the following expression constructs:
        • "foo" - Single arguments.
        • "foo,bar,baz" - Multiple OR'ed arguments.
        • "foo | bar | baz" - Multiple OR'ed arguments, pipe syntax.
        • "foo || bar || baz" - Multiple OR'ed arguments, Java-OR syntax.
        • "fo*" - Patterns including '*' and '?'.
        • "fo* & *oo" - Multiple AND'ed arguments, ampersand syntax.
        • "fo* && *oo" - Multiple AND'ed arguments, Java-AND syntax.
        • "fo* || (*oo || bar)" - Parenthesis.
      • AND operations take precedence over OR operations (as expected).
      • Whitespace is ignored.
      • null or empty expressions always match as false.
      • If patterns are used, you must specify the list of declared roles using rolesDeclared().
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • rolesDeclared

      String rolesDeclared
      Declared roles.

      A comma-delimited list of all possible user roles.

      Used in conjunction with roleGuard() is used with patterns.

      This is a shortcut for specifying RestOp.rolesDeclared() on all the REST operations on a class.

      Example:

      @Rest( rolesDeclared="ROLE_ADMIN,ROLE_READ_WRITE,ROLE_READ_ONLY,ROLE_SPECIAL", roleGuard="ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE && ROLE_SPECIAL)" ) public class MyResource extends BasicRestServlet { ... }

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • serializers

      Class<? extends Serializer>[] serializers
      Specifies the serializers for POJOs into HTTP response bodies.

      Serializer are used to convert POJOs to HTTP response bodies.
      Any of the Juneau framework serializers can be used in this setting.
      The serializer selected is based on the request Accept header matched against the values returned by the following method using a best-match algorithm:

      • Serializer.getMediaTypeRanges()

      Serializers are automatically inherited from serializers() annotations on parent classes with the serializers on child classes prepended to the serializer group. The SerializerSet.NoInherit class can be used to prevent inheriting from the parent class.

      Example:

      // Define a REST resource that can produce JSON and XML. @Rest( serializers={ JsonParser.class, XmlParser.class } ) public class MyResource { ... }

      The serializers can also be tailored at the method level using RestOp.serializers() (and related annotations).

      For programmatic equivalents, contribute a SerializerSet bean via @Bean(name="serializers").

      Inheritance Rules
      • Serializers on child override those on parent class.
      • SerializerSet.Inherit class can be used to inherit and augment values from parent.
      • SerializerSet.NoInherit class can be used to suppress inheriting from parent.
      • Serializers on methods take precedence over those on classes.
      See Also:
      • Marshalling
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • siteName

      String siteName
      Optional site name.

      The site name is intended to be a title that can be applied to the entire site.

      One possible use is if you want to add the same title to the top of all pages by defining a header on a common parent class like so:

      @HtmlDocConfig( header={ "<h1>$RS{siteName}</h1>", "<h2>$RS{title}</h2>" } )

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • staticFiles

      Class<? extends StaticFiles> staticFiles
      Static files.

      Used to retrieve localized files to be served up as static files through the REST API via the following predefined methods:

      • BasicRestResource.getHtdoc(String, Locale).
      • BasicRestServlet.getHtdoc(String, Locale).

      The static file finder can be accessed through the following methods:

      • RestContext.getStaticFiles()
      Inheritance Rules
      • Static files on child are combined with those on parent class.
      • Static files are are executed child-to-parent in the order they appear in the annotation.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.rest.server.staticfile.StaticFiles.Void.class

    • swagger

      Swagger swagger
      Provides swagger-specific metadata on this resource.

      Used to populate the auto-generated OPTIONS swagger documentation.

      Example:

      @Rest( path="/addressBook", // Swagger info. swagger=@Swagger({ "contact:{name:'John Smith',email:'john@smith.com'},", "license:{name:'Apache 2.0',url:'http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html'},", "version:'2.0',, "termsOfService:'You are on your own.',", "tags:[{name:'Java',description:'Java utility',externalDocs:{description:'Home page',url:'http://juneau.apache.org'}}],", "externalDocs:{description:'Home page',url:'http://juneau.apache.org'}" }) )

      See Also:
      • Swagger
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      @org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Swagger

    • swaggerProvider

      Class<? extends SwaggerProvider> swaggerProvider
      Swagger provider.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.rest.server.swagger.SwaggerProvider.Void.class

    • openApiProvider

      Class<? extends OpenApiProvider> openApiProvider
      OpenAPI 3.1 provider.

      Sibling of swaggerProvider() for the OpenAPI 3.1 emission path. Defaults to OpenApiProvider.Void which resolves to BasicOpenApiProvider unless the bean store provides an override.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.rest.server.openapi.OpenApiProvider.Void.class

    • title

      String[] title
      Optional servlet title.

      It is used to populate the Swagger title field.

      Inheritance Rules
      • Label is searched for in child-to-parent order.
      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      • Corresponds to the swagger field /info/title.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • uriAuthority

      String uriAuthority
      Resource authority path.

      Overrides the authority path value for this resource and any child resources.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • uriContext

      String uriContext
      Resource context path.

      Overrides the context path value for this resource and any child resources.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • uriRelativity

      String uriRelativity
      URI-resolution relativity.

      Specifies how relative URIs should be interpreted by serializers.

      See UriResolution for possible values.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • uriResolution

      String uriResolution
      URI-resolution.

      Specifies how relative URIs should be interpreted by serializers.

      See UriResolution for possible values.

      Notes:
      • Supports SVL Variables (e.g. "$L{my.localized.variable}").
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

Copyright © 2016–2026 The Apache Software Foundation. All rights reserved.
